message: tool-error rate over the recent window crossed 25% — stop retrying variations; re-read the failing tool's contract (params, required prior calls like Read-before-Edit, cwd state) before the next attempt

a hook appears to have exited non-zero, blocked a call, or leaked a traceback — hooks MUST exit 0 on every path and emit their stdout payload exactly once at the end. Run `python3 skills/compliance-canary/tools/hook_validate.py` to find the offending entrypoint; wrap slow work in skills/compliance-canary/tools/deadline.py (hard_deadline / run_or_exit0).

new build machinery (solver/cache/gate/orchestrator/pipeline) was written without a borrow-checkpoint line — state in one line which existing framework/library/tool was checked and why it doesn't fit before commissioning bespoke infrastructure. Building bespoke after a real check is a legitimate pass; skipping the check is the Reinvented Wheel failure mode.

a sub-frontier lane was briefed to diagnose ('investigate why / figure out the cause') — diagnosis is frontier-tier work. Reproduce the defect at the artifact layer yourself, name the root cause, prescribe the fix, then delegate the spec'd execution (ORCHESTRATION.md §6 SPEC'D×GATED routing). Mechanical evidence-gathering sweeps remain delegable.

>=3 failing tool results accumulated in the recent window — whether one failure is recurring or several distinct things are breaking, the task is stalling. Before the next attempt: (1) restate the goal/scope, (2) re-gather evidence about WHY each failure happens (the tool's contract, the actual state, the exact error text — don't lump distinct failures together), (3) adversarially falsify your current theory — if two attempts at the same fix failed, the diagnosis is wrong, (4) verify the fix fresh, (5) report calibrated.
_note: Rehomed from retired fable-mode (2026-07-19 catalog contraction); detector semantics and locked lesson unchanged. MECHANISM, stated honestly: detect_repeated_tool_error COUNTS is_error tool_results matching the pattern (>=min_count in the recent window); it does NOT group by signature and cannot tell 'same failure 3x' from '3 unrelated failures' — so the message claims only 'the task is stalling', true in both cases. Locked lesson: a probe's message must not assert semantics its detector cannot observe. COMPLEMENTARY to the always-on trajectory_drift (rate errs/calls>=0.25 over >=8 calls): this fires on absolute COUNT >=3 at any rate. Blind to keyword-free failures (bare 'Killed', non-English, exit-0-wrong-output) — accepted scope (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3). MEASURED (2026-07-07, product-images repo, 32 sessions / 783 is_error results): 87.7% of error texts match; severity=warn nudge, consumer repos may raise min_count in their fork.

message: dependency manifest/lockfile changed — every dependency is permanent code you don't control. Confirm stdlib/existing can't do it, and state WHY the new dep is needed.
_note: Rehomed from retired lean-execution (2026-07-19 catalog contraction); cited by the resident code-craft directives (surgical diffs).

message: an Edit changed only whitespace/formatting — don't reformat code you didn't change; a formatter pass buries the real lines. Keep the diff to the task.
_note: Rehomed from retired lean-execution (2026-07-19 catalog contraction); cited by the resident code-craft directives (surgical diffs).

message: you have open user requests this session but no sign of maintaining a visible ledger (no Edit/Write/NotebookEdit to .brainer/ledger/<sid>.md and no TaskCreate/TaskUpdate) in the recent window — materialize or refresh .brainer/ledger/<sid>.md so an item the user raised is not silently dropped. Keep one row per user request/question/sub-item (decompose each user message into atomic rows). NEVER DELETE A ROW — only re-status it (open/doing/done/blocked); a row that vanishes between edits is the same silent-drop failure this probe exists to catch.
_note: Rehomed from retired requirements-ledger (2026-07-19 catalog contraction). SKILL.md's 'never delete a row — only re-status' rule has no dedicated drift probe: none of hook.py's implemented DETECTORS kinds can diff ledger content ACROSS turns to notice a row disappearing (forbidden_regex/claim_without_evidence etc. only see current-window text, not a structural diff of a markdown table over time). Rather than invent an unimplemented kind (the canary hook would silently skip it — an alive-looking-dead gate), the rule is folded into this probe's message so at least the maintenance-absence case still surfaces it. Honest degradation, not full mechanical coverage.

message: this done-status line names something the USER will do (or a deferred/third-person action) — that is a self-close on an interpretation you never got confirmed, not a verified done. Live incident: 'done — byte-identical copy (scaffold; Saar sorts out art)' shipped a scaffold as done while quietly assuming the user would separately do the art pass. Covers done/complete/finished/closed claims, no-parens phrasing, one sentence-boundary crossing, and any third-person verb ('handles', 'sorts') or 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' deferral — but not when a strong evidence token (verified/N-of-N/render/test/manifest/matched) appears BETWEEN the done-word and the assumption phrase (position-bound: trailing evidence AFTER the assumption clause does not suppress). A future/deferral marker ('tomorrow', 'next') anywhere in the same done-line OVERRIDES the evidence-token suppression outright — evidence of past work never excuses a still-deferred user action, so 'done — tests pass; Saar sorts out art tomorrow' fires even though 'tests' is a strong evidence token. Present-confirmation verbs (agrees, confirms, approves, accepts, acknowledges, signs off) are excluded from the third-person-verb generalization — those describe a completed confirmation, not a deferred/future user action, so 'user agrees this is complete' and 'Saar confirms receipt' stay silent. _note: threat model is unintentional drift; a deliberately fabricated evidence token is verify-before-completion's domain, not this regex. Re-status as doing/deferred until the assumption is confirmed, or state the assumption OUTSIDE the done-claim and ask before closing.
_note: Rehomed from retired requirements-ledger (2026-07-19 catalog contraction). ADVISORY heuristic over unbounded natural language — observed-incident phrasings + common variants are covered and regression-tested (3 adversarial rounds); paraphrase false-negatives and topic-collision false-positives remain possible by design and are accepted (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3: heuristic banked with scope of validity). Residual (accepted, not fixed here): 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' are NOT added to the deferral-override lookahead alongside 'tomorrow'/'next' — a locked regression case ('done — verified 31/31 via manifest (literal phrase user will was covered by the negative test)') requires a quoted/attributive mention of 'will' to stay silent when evidence is present, and a bare-word override for 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' would collide with that quoted-mention case (both are indistinguishable to a regex). A genuine deferred close using 'later'/'afterwards'/'will' phrasing WITH intervening evidence text will therefore still be silenced — an accepted false-negative narrower than the 'tomorrow'/'next' fix.
